notes: Official Monarch product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
I can’t see your cards’ issuers, networks, or rewards setups from your Monarch data, so I can’t auto‑build a precise, card‑by‑card routing plan yet.

We can still get you there if you’re up for sharing a bit more detail. Copy/paste this and fill it in for each card you actually use:

Card #1
Issuer & card name:
Network (Visa/Mastercard/Amex/Discover):
Personal or business:
Key rewards: (e.g., 4x dining, 3x groceries, 3x travel, 1x everything else)
Notable perks (if any): (e.g., travel protections, software credits, office‑supply bonus, etc.)
Card #2
Issuer & card name:
Network:
Personal or business:
Key rewards:
Notable perks:

…keep going for each card you actually use.

Once you send that, I’ll turn it into a simple routing table tailored to your setup, like:

Spending Category | Primary Card | Why | Backup Card
------------------|-------------|-----|------------
Software/SaaS     | …           | …   | …
Business travel   | …           | …   | …
Groceries         | …           | …   | …
Dining            | …           | …   | …
Office supplies   | …           | …   | …

If you’d rather not list specific cards, I can give you general rules instead (like “use your highest‑multiplier travel card for flights/hotels, grocery card for supermarkets, etc.”), it’ll just be less precise.

Which route do you want to take?
